These tombs are adorned with wooden statues, representing with perfect exactness the tattoo of the deceased. His books, his herbals, his set of pigeon-holes, his instruments of precision, his chemical apparatus, his collection of thermometers, barometers, hygrometers, rain-gauges, spectacles, compasses, sextants, maps, plans, flasks, powders, bottles for medicine-chest, were all classed in an order that would have shamed the British Museum. The space of six square feet contained incalculable riches: the doctor had only to stretch out his hand without moving to become instantaneously a doctor, a mathematician, an astronomer, a geographer, a botanist, or a conchologist. It must be acknowledged that he was proud of his management and happy in his floating sanctuary, which three of his thinnest friends would have sufficed to fill. The Giant Telescope is a colossal reflecting telescope commissioned by the [[Gun Club]] to track the flight of the [[Lunar Projectile]] after it was fired from the [[Columbiad]] toward the Moon. Erected atop Long's Peak in the Rocky Mountains, it was, in the world of *[[from-the-earth-to-the-moon|From the Earth to the Moon]]*, the most powerful astronomical instrument ever built.
